text: Publish placeholder kalamu@0.0.1 to npm to reserve the name #publishing
Kalamu roadmap (post-MVP)
CLI
next --under <id> and --include-handed-off flags #cli
unhandoff command #cli
list --depth, show --depth, show --format markdown #cli
Add ability in #cli to pass a flag that brings up the next x number of tasks or even --all - that way these can all be loaded into context at once for the agent to work on

When running CLI next - the returned data should also return direct ancestors so that the AI has full context. Eg - in the case of this task it should also return CLI (the parent) and (Kalamu Roadmap) the grandparent. Not the siblings or the siblings descendants. So just the full descendant tree and direct ancestors #cli
Web UI
Tag filter action from the chip colour popover #web
Come up with an alternative set of keyboard shortcuts where we currently have Alt + X - they are too widely used in mac for other stuff. E.g. Aerospace in my case
When text spans only one line (so not full width) of the #web gui, then the 'click to focus' surface area is only on the text - it should instead span the full width
Rebind or document Cmd+M (self toggle): some macOS browsers reserve it for window minimize #web
Drag-selection starting on an unfocused node begins an edit at the click point instead of selecting; consider selection support in the display rendering #web
Unify web copy.ts serializer with CLI renderMarkdown (identical format, two implementations) #web #cli
Add command to command palette to copy CLI commands - the UI has the id of the node so this is easily done:
Root level command label is `Copy CLI command` - then sub-menu options can list all relevant commands to copy to clipboard
Add keyboard shortcut Mod + Shift + C - which copies id of currently focused node (if any) to clipboard
Distribution
Write README with install and agent-guide sections before publishing #publishing
Verify on Windows: fs.watch, browser open command, atomic renames #publishing

Confirm one-Esc palette close in your own browser — the reported blur-without-close was not reproducible in Chromium (suspect Safari or an extension eating Esc); Esc is now handled at window capture so it should hold regardless #bug
I am on Chromium and this is still an issue - specifically - it requires two Escapes to close the dialog. First one blurs input, second one closes the command palette.
Re-test in your browser: blur-to-body now closes the palette in one Esc even with Esc-eating extensions (Overlay closes on focus-leave while the window stays focused)
Before publishing the real CLI: resolve @kalamu/core workspace:* dep (publish core too, or bundle it into cli/dist) and bump version >=0.1.0 #publishing

For an empty node, we ignore 'Enter' keypresses because that would just create a new empty node, if the node in question is indented, we mimic the same as Shift+Tab to unindent it. Instead, have it so that pressing 'Enter' on an empty node toggles task mode #web
Should text nodes have
Agent skill: skills/kalamu/SKILL.md (agentskills.io spec) + kalamu init offers npx skills add install #cli #agents
Landing site (Astro, packages/landing)
Scaffold packages/landing with Astro (minimal, static output) #landing
Design + write real landing content (hero, live outline demo island, install, agent story) #landing
Deploy landing to kalamu.dev (CF Pages or Vercel, root dir packages/landing) + DNS at registrar #landing
Hero CTA: 'Copy instructions for agent' button — copies an agent prompt that scaffolds Kalamu setup (init, skill install, gitignore, verify) and then explains usage to the user #landing
kalamu init --tour: seed a self-guided onboarding outline (all tasks self so agents never pick them up); landing CTA prompt uses it and ends with kalamu open #cli #landing
